diagnosis:
1. "read-image-file" (80%): read_media_file's description doesn't state that it already returns full content in one call, so the agent sometimes adds a redundant get_file_info call "to be safe" about the file before/after reading it. The description doesn't explicitly say "no need to check file info first/after."

2. "compare-two-configs" (0%): read_multiple_files description says "more efficient than reading files one by one" but doesn't strongly instruct the agent to PREFER it whenever 2+ files are requested/compared. Meanwhile read_text_file's description explicitly says "Use this tool when you need to examine the contents of a single file" — but the agent still chose it twice in sequence for two files instead of recognizing the multi-file tool. The single-file framing on read_text_file isn't strong enough of a contrast, and read_multiple_files doesn't lead with "use this instead of calling read_text_file multiple times when you need 2+ files."

3. "create-new-file" (40%): create_directory's description says "Perfect for setting up directory structures for projects or ensuring required paths exist," which primes the agent to believe it must always ensure the parent directory exists before writing a file. write_file's description doesn't clarify that it already creates any needed parent directories, so the agent defensively calls create_directory first.

4. "make-nested-directory" (0%): create_directory's description doesn't state that it is idempotent/atomic for nested paths in a single call and doesn't discourage verifying step-by-step. The agent repeatedly calls create_directory on parent and child paths and even calls list_allowed_directories, because the description doesn't reassure that ONE call with the full nested path is sufficient and safe (silent success first, so it's unclear whether it actually succeeded, prompting checks/retries).
</diagnosis>
<rewrites>
[
{"tool": "read_media_file", "new_description": "Read an image or audio file and return its full content as a base64-encoded block with MIME type (or as an embedded resource for other types) in a single call. This already returns complete content — do not call get_file_info or any other tool before or after it. Only works within allowed directories.", "rationale": "Stops the agent from adding a redundant get_file_info call after successfully reading the media file."},
{"tool": "read_multiple_files", "new_description": "Read the contents of two or more files in one call. ALWAYS use this instead of calling read_text_file multiple times whenever a task involves comparing, reviewing, or reading more than one file — it is faster and each file's content is labeled with its path. Failed reads for individual files won't stop the entire operation. Only works within allowed directories.", "rationale": "Makes it explicit that any multi-file request must route to this tool instead of repeated read_text_file calls."},
{"tool": "read_text_file", "new_description": "Read the complete contents of a SINGLE file from the file system as text. Use the 'head' parameter to read only the first N lines, or 'tail' for the last N lines. If you need to read or compare two or more files, use read_multiple_files instead of calling this tool repeatedly. Only works within allowed directories.", "rationale": "Explicitly redirects multi-file requests to read_multiple_files instead of chaining single-file reads."},
{"tool": "write_file", "new_description": "Create a new file or completely overwrite an existing file with new content; automatically creates any missing parent directories, so you do NOT need to call create_directory first. Use with caution as it overwrites existing files without warning. Only works within allowed directories.", "rationale": "Removes the agent's belief that it must pre-create parent directories before writing a file."},
{"tool": "create_directory", "new_description": "Create a directory at the given path, including any missing parent directories, in a single call — nested paths like 'a/b/c' are created atomically and only need one call. If the directory already exists, this succeeds silently with no output; do not call it again or verify with list_directory/list_allowed_directories afterward. Only works within allowed directories.", "rationale": "Clarifies one call suffices for nested paths and discourages redundant verification/repeat calls."}
]
</rewrites>
</propose_rewrites>
